     9	};

    23	
    24	#[inline]
    25	pub(crate) fn parse_declaration_or_rule_list_block(p: &mut CssParser) -> CompletedMarker {
    26	    DeclarationOrRuleListBlock.parse_block_body(p)
    27	}
    28	
    29	struct DeclarationOrRuleListBlock;
    30	
    31	impl ParseBlockBody for DeclarationOrRuleListBlock {
    32	    const BLOCK_KIND: CssSyntaxKind = CSS_DECLARATION_OR_RULE_BLOCK;
    33	
    34	    fn is_at_element(&self, p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
    35	        is_at_declaration_or_rule_item(p)
    36	    }
    37	
    38	    fn parse_list(&mut self, p: &mut CssParser) {
    39	        DeclarationOrRuleList::new(T!['}']).parse_list(p);
    40	    }
    41	}
    42	
    43	#[inline]
    44	fn is_at_declaration_or_rule_item(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
    45	    is_at_at_rule(p)
    46	        || is_at_nested_qualified_rule(p)
    47	        || is_at_scss_nesting_declaration(p)
    48	        || is_at_scss_declaration(p)
    49	        || is_at_scss_interpolated_property(p)
    50	        || is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p)
    51	        || is_at_metavariable(p)
    52	}
    53	
    54	struct DeclarationOrRuleListParseRecovery {
    55	    end_kind: CssSyntaxKind,
    56	}
    57	
    58	impl DeclarationOrRuleListParseRecovery {
    59	    fn new(end_kind: CssSyntaxKind) -> Self {
    60	        Self { end_kind }
    61	    }
    62	}
    63	
    64	impl ParseRecovery for DeclarationOrRuleListParseRecovery {
    65	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
    66	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
    67	    const RECOVERED_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_BOGUS;
    68	
    69	    fn is_at_recovered(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
    70	        p.at(self.end_kind) || is_at_declaration_or_rule_item(p)
    71	    }
    72	}
    73	
    74	pub(crate) struct DeclarationOrRuleList {
    75	    end_kind: CssSyntaxKind,
    76	}
    77	
    78	impl DeclarationOrRuleList {
    79	    pub(crate) fn new(end_kind: CssSyntaxKind) -> Self {
    80	        Self { end_kind }
    81	    }
    82	}
    83	
    84	impl ParseNodeList for DeclarationOrRuleList {
    85	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
    86	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
    87	    const LIST_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_DECLARATION_OR_RULE_LIST;
    88	
    89	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> ParsedSyntax {
    90	        if is_at_at_rule(p) {
    91	            parse_at_rule(p)
    92	        } else if is_at_scss_nesting_declaration(p) {
    93	            // Match Sass's declaration-first strategy for ambiguous `name:ident` and
    94	            // `name::...` forms, but do the ambiguity check inside the same speculative
    95	            // declaration parse so we don't pay a separate probe pass first.
    96	            if let Ok(declaration) = try_parse_scss_nesting_declaration(p, self.end_kind) {
    97	                return declaration;
    98	            }
    99	
   100	            if let Ok(rule) =
   101	                try_parse_nested_qualified_rule_without_selector_recovery(p, self.end_kind)
   102	            {
   103	                return rule;
   104	            }
   105	
   106	            parse_scss_nesting_declaration(p)
   107	        } else if is_at_scss_declaration(p) {
   108	            CssSyntaxFeatures::Scss.parse_exclusive_syntax(
   109	                p,
   110	                parse_scss_declaration,
   111	                |p, marker| {
   112	                    scss_only_syntax_error(p, "SCSS variable declarations", marker.range(p))
   113	                },
   114	            )
   115	        } else if is_at_scss_interpolated_property(p) {
   116	            // The remaining interpolation-bearing property names here are the
   117	            // dashed/custom-property forms that `is_at_scss_nesting_declaration`
   118	            // intentionally excludes, such as `--theme-#{$slot}: red;`.
   119	            if let Ok(rule) =
   120	                try_parse_nested_qualified_rule_without_selector_recovery(p, self.end_kind)
   121	            {
   122	                return rule;
   123	            }
   124	
   125	            parse_declaration_with_semicolon(p)
   126	        } else if is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p) {
   127	            // if we are at a declaration,
   128	            // we still can have a nested qualified rule or a declaration
   129	            // E.g.
   130	            // main {
   131	            //     label:hover {  <---
   132	            // it looks like a declaration but it is a nested qualified rule
   133	            //         font-weight: 500;
   134	            //     }
   135	            // }
   136	
   137	            // Reset the if-function flag before parsing. This flag is used to detect
   138	            // if we encountered an if() function during speculative parsing.
   139	            p.state_mut().encountered_if_function = false;
   140	
   141	            // Attempt to parse the current block as a declaration.
   142	            let declaration = try_parse(p, |p| {
   143	                let declaration = parse_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p);
   144	
   145	                // If we encountered an if() function, always fail speculative parsing.
   146	                // The if() function uses semicolons as branch separators, which can cause
   147	                // p.last() to be `;` even when the declaration is incomplete. By failing
   148	                // here, we force a non-speculative retry where recovery is enabled.
   149	                if p.state().encountered_if_function {
   150	                    return Err(());
   151	                }
   152	
   153	                // Check if the *last* token parsed is a semicolon
   154	                // (;) or if the parser is at a closing brace (}).
   155	                // ; - Indicates the end of a declaration.
   156	                // Indicates the end of the last declaration because `;` is optional.
   157	                // E.g
   158	                // .class {
   159	                //     color: red; <----
   160	                // The semicolon indicates the end of the declaration.
   161	                //     font-size: 16px
   162	                // } <---
   163	                // The closing brace indicates the end of the declaration block.
   164	                // If either condition is true, the declaration is considered valid.
   165	                let valid = matches!(p.last(), Some(T![;])) || p.at(self.end_kind);
   166	                if valid { Ok(declaration) } else { Err(()) }
   167	            });
   168	
   169	            // If parsing as a declaration was successful, return the parsed declaration.
   170	            if let Ok(declaration) = declaration {
   171	                return declaration;
   172	            }
   173	
   174	            // If the speculative parse failed and we encountered an if() function,
   175	            // we know this is a declaration (not a rule) because if() can only appear
   176	            // in declaration values. Parse again with recovery enabled.
   177	            if std::mem::take(&mut p.state_mut().encountered_if_function) {
   178	                return parse_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p);
   179	            }
   180	
   181	            // If parsing as a declaration failed,
   182	            // attempt to parse the current block as a nested qualified rule.
   183	            let rule = try_parse(p, |p| {
   184	                // Parse the block as a nested qualified rule.
   185	                let rule = parse_nested_qualified_rule(p);
   186	                // Check if the *last* token parsed is a closing brace (}).
   187	                // Indicates the end of a rule block.
   188	                // If true, the nested qualified rule is considered valid.
   189	                if p.last().is_some_and(|kind| kind == self.end_kind) || p.at(self.end_kind) {
   190	                    Ok(rule)
   191	                } else {
   192	                    // If the condition is not met, return an error to indicate parsing failure.
   193	                    Err(())
   194	                }
   195	            });
   196	
   197	            // If parsing as a nested qualified rule was successful, return the parsed rule.
   198	            if let Ok(rule) = rule {
   199	                return rule;
   200	            }
   201	
   202	            // If both parsing attempts fail,
   203	            // fall back to parsing the block as a declaration,
   204	            // because declaration error is more relevant.
   205	            parse_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p)
   206	        } else if is_at_nested_qualified_rule(p) {
   207	            parse_nested_qualified_rule(p)
   208	        } else if is_at_metavariable(p) {
   209	            parse_metavariable(p)
   210	        } else {
   211	            Absent
   212	        }
   213	    }
   214	
   215	    fn is_at_list_end(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   216	        p.at(self.end_kind)
   217	    }
   218	
   219	    fn recover(
   220	        &mut self,
   221	        p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>,
   222	        parsed_element: ParsedSyntax,
   223	    ) -> RecoveryResult {
   224	        parsed_element.or_recover(
   225	            p,
   226	            &DeclarationOrRuleListParseRecovery::new(self.end_kind),
   227	            expected_any_declaration_or_at_rule,
   228	        )
   229	    }
   230	}
   231

2026-06-01 05:29:25,568 - INFO - swea-agent-biomejs__biome-9995 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I can see the issue clearly. It's about parsing CSS-in-JS template literals. The problem is:

1. **Works**: When a property (`background: black;`) comes BEFORE a nested CSS block (`div:first-of-type { ... }`), it parses correctly.
2. **Does not work**: When the nested CSS block comes BEFORE a property, it fails to parse.

The issue is in the `declaration_or_rule_list_block.rs` file. When `is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p)` is true (which includes cases like `div:first-of-type {`), it first tries to parse as a declaration, and if that fails, it tries to parse as a nested qualified rule.

But the problem is: `div:first-of-type {` could be mistaken for a declaration (property: value) because it starts with `div:first-of-type`. Let me trace through the logic.

Let me look at how `is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon` works

    45	pub(crate) enum CssSyntaxFeatures {
    46	    /// Enable support for SCSS-specific syntax.
    47	    Scss,
    48	    /// Enable support for Tailwind CSS directives and syntax.
    49	    Tailwind,
    50	
    51	    /// Enable support for CSS Modules syntax.
    52	    CssModules,
    53	
    54	    /// Enable support for CSS Modules syntax plus parsing of pseudo selectors for `:slotted`, `:deep`, and the `v-bind()` function.
    55	    CssModulesWithVue,
    56	}
    57	
    58	pub(crate) use declaration::{
    59	    DeclarationList, is_at_any_declaration, is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon,
    60	    is_at_declaration, parse_any_declaration_with_semicolon, parse_declaration_with_value_end_set,
    61	};
    62	
    63	impl SyntaxFeature for CssSyntaxFeatures {
    64	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
    65	
    66	    fn is_supported(&self, p: &Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
    67	        match self {
    68	            Self::Scss => p.source_type.is_scss(),
    69	            Self::Tailwind => p.options().is_tailwind_directives_enabled(),
    70	            Self::CssModules => p.options().is_css_modules_enabled(),
    71	            Self::CssModulesWithVue => p.options().is_css_modules_vue_enabled(),
    72	        }
    73	    }
    74	}
    75	
    76	pub(crate) fn parse_root(p: &mut CssParser) {
    77	    let m = p.start();
    78	    match p.source_type.as_embedding_kind() {
    79	        EmbeddingKind::Styled => {
    80	            DeclarationOrRuleList::new(EOF).parse_list(p);
    81	
    82	            m.complete(p, CSS_SNIPPET_ROOT);
    83	        }
    84	        EmbeddingKind::None | EmbeddingKind::Html(_) => {
    85	            p.eat(UNICODE_BOM);
    86	
    87	            RootItemList.parse_list(p);
    88	
    89	            m.complete(p, CSS_ROOT);
    90	        }
    91	    }
    92	}
    93	
    94	struct RootItemList;
    95	
    96	#[inline]
    97	pub(crate) fn is_at_root_item_list_element(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
    98	    is_at_at_rule(p) || is_at_scss_declaration(p) || is_at_qualified_rule(p)
    99	}
   100	
   101	struct RootItemListParseRecovery;
   102	
   103	impl ParseRecovery for RootItemListParseRecovery {
   104	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   105	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   106	    const RECOVERED_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_BOGUS_RULE;
   107	
   108	    fn is_at_recovered(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   109	        p.at(EOF) || is_at_root_item_list_element(p)
   110	    }
   111	}
   112	
   113	impl ParseNodeList for RootItemList {
   114	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   115	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   116	    const LIST_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_ROOT_ITEM_LIST;
   117	
   118	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> ParsedSyntax {
   119	        if is_at_at_rule(p) {
   120	            parse_at_rule(p)
   121	        } else if is_at_scss_declaration(p) {
   122	            CssSyntaxFeatures::Scss.parse_exclusive_syntax(
   123	                p,
   124	                parse_scss_declaration,
   125	                |p, marker| {
   126	                    scss_only_syntax_error(p, "SCSS variable declarations", marker.range(p))
   127	                },
   128	            )
   129	        } else if is_at_qualified_rule(p) {
   130	            parse_qualified_rule(p)
   131	        } else {
   132	            Absent
   133	        }
   134	    }
   135	
   136	    fn is_at_list_end(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   137	        p.at(EOF)
   138	    }
   139	
   140	    fn recover(
   141	        &mut self,
   142	        p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>,
   143	        parsed_element: ParsedSyntax,
   144	    ) -> RecoveryResult {
   145	        parsed_element.or_recover(p, &RootItemListParseRecovery, expected_any_rule)
   146	    }
   147	}
   148	
   149	struct RuleList {
   150	    end_kind: CssSyntaxKind,
   151	}
   152	
   153	impl RuleList {
   154	    fn new(end_kind: CssSyntaxKind) -> Self {
   155	        Self { end_kind }
   156	    }
   157	}
   158	
   159	#[inline]
   160	pub(crate) fn is_at_rule_list_element(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   161	    is_at_at_rule(p) || is_at_qualified_rule(p)
   162	}
   163	
   164	struct RuleListParseRecovery {
   165	    end_kind: CssSyntaxKind,
   166	}
   167	
   168	impl RuleListParseRecovery {
   169	    fn new(end_kind: CssSyntaxKind) -> Self {
   170	        Self { end_kind }
   171	    }
   172	}
   173	
   174	impl ParseRecovery for RuleListParseRecovery {
   175	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   176	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   177	    const RECOVERED_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_BOGUS_RULE;
   178	
   179	    fn is_at_recovered(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   180	        p.at(self.end_kind) || is_at_rule_list_element(p)
   181	    }
   182	}
   183	
   184	impl ParseNodeList for RuleList {
   185	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   186	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   187	    const LIST_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_RULE_LIST;
   188	
   189	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> ParsedSyntax {
   190	        if is_at_at_rule(p) {
   191	            parse_at_rule(p)
   192	        } else if is_at_qualified_rule(p) {
   193	            parse_qualified_rule(p)
   194	        } else {
   195	            Absent
   196	        }
   197	    }
   198	
   199	    fn is_at_list_end(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   200	        p.at(self.end_kind)
   201	    }
   202	
   203	    fn recover(
   204	        &mut self,
   205	        p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>,
   206	        parsed_element: ParsedSyntax,
   207	    ) -> RecoveryResult {
   208	        parsed_element.or_recover(
   209	            p,
   210	            &RuleListParseRecovery::new(self.end_kind),
   211	            expected_any_rule,
   212	        )
   213	    }
   214	}
   215	
   216	#[inline]
   217	pub(crate) fn is_at_qualified_rule(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   218	    is_nth_at_selector(p, 0)
   219	}
   220	
   221	#[inline]
   222	pub(crate) fn parse_qualified_rule(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   223	    if !is_at_qualified_rule(p) {
   224	        return Absent;
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    let m = p.start();
   228	
   229	    SelectorList::default().parse_list(p);
   230	
   231	    parse_declaration_or_rule_list_block(p);
   232	
   233	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_QUALIFIED_RULE))
   234	}
   235	
   236	/// Checks if the current position in the CSS parser is at the start of a nested qualified rule.
   237	/// Nested qualified rules are determined by the presence of a relative selector, indicating the
   238	/// start of a rule that is nested within another rule.
   239	#[inline]
   240	pub(crate) fn is_at_nested_qualified_rule(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   241	    is_at_relative_selector(p)
   242	}
   243	
   244	/// Parses a nested qualified rule from the current position in the CSS parser. If the current
   245	/// position is identified as the start of a nested qualified rule, it proceeds to parse the rule.
   246	/// This involves parsing the list of relative selectors and then parsing or recovering the declaration
   247	/// or rule list block. The kind of rule parsed (nested qualified or bogus) is determined based on
   248	/// the success of parsing the block.
   249	#[inline]
   250	pub(crate) fn parse_nested_qualified_rule(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   251	    parse_nested_qualified_rule_with_selector_recovery(p, false).map_or(Absent, |(rule, _)| rule)
   252	}
   253	
   254	/// Speculatively parses a nested qualified rule without selector recovery.
   255	///
   256	/// This is used to disambiguate SCSS nesting declarations from nested qualified rules.
   257	/// The parse is considered successful only when the selector is strict and the parsed
   258	/// block is complete.
   259	#[inline]
   260	pub(crate) fn try_parse_nested_qualified_rule_without_selector_recovery(
   261	    p: &mut CssParser,
   262	    end_kind: CssSyntaxKind,
   263	) -> Result<ParsedSyntax, ()> {
   264	    try_parse(p, |p| {
   265	        let Some((rule, block_kind)) = parse_nested_qualified_rule_with_selector_recovery(p, true)
   266	        else {
   267	            return Err(());
   268	        };
   269	
   270	        if block_kind != CSS_DECLARATION_OR_RULE_BLOCK
   271	            || p.last().is_none_or(|kind| kind != end_kind)
   272	        {
   273	            return Err(());
   274	        }
   275	
   276	        Ok(rule)
   277	    })
   278	}
   279	
   280	#[inline]
   281	fn parse_nested_qualified_rule_with_selector_recovery(
   282	    p: &mut CssParser,
   283	    disable_selector_recovery: bool,
   284	) -> Option<(ParsedSyntax, CssSyntaxKind)> {
   285	    if !is_at_nested_qualified_rule(p) {
   286	        return None;
   287	    }
   288	
   289	    let m = p.start();
   290	
   291	    if disable_selector_recovery {
   292	        RelativeSelectorList::new(T!['{'])
   293	            .disable_recovery()
   294	            .parse_list(p);
   295	
   296	        // In strict mode, reject selectors that don't reach the opening brace.
   297	        if !p.at(T!['{']) {
   298	            m.abandon(p);
   299	            return None;
   300	        }
   301	    } else {
   302	        RelativeSelectorList::new(T!['{']).parse_list(p);
   303	    }
   304	
   305	    let block = parse_declaration_or_rule_list_block(p);
   306	    let block_kind = block.kind(p);
   307	
   308	    Some((
   309	        Present(m.complete(p, CSS_NESTED_QUALIFIED_RULE)),
   310	        block_kind,
   311	    ))
   312	}
   313	
   314	#[inline]
   315	fn is_at_metavariable(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   316	    p.at(GRIT_METAVARIABLE)
   317	}
   318	
   319	#[inline]
   320	fn is_nth_at_metavariable(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   321	    p.nth_at(n, GRIT_METAVARIABLE)
   322	}
   323	
   324	#[inline]
   325	fn parse_metavariable(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   326	    if !is_at_metavariable(p) {
   327	        return Absent;
   328	    }
   329	    let m = p.start();
   330	    p.bump(GRIT_METAVARIABLE);
   331	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_METAVARIABLE))
   332	}
   333	
   334	#[inline]
   335	pub(crate) fn is_at_any_value(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   336	    is_at_any_value_with_mode(p, ValueParsingMode::ScssAware)
   337	}
   338	
   339	/// Checks if the parser is at the start of any value for the provided parsing mode.
   340	#[inline]
   341	pub(crate) fn is_at_any_value_with_mode(p: &mut CssParser, mode: ValueParsingMode) -> bool {
   342	    is_at_any_value_with_context(p, ValueParsingContext::new(p, mode))
   343	}
   344	
   345	#[inline]
   346	pub(crate) fn is_at_any_value_with_context(
   347	    p: &mut CssParser,
   348	    context: ValueParsingContext,
   349	) -> bool {
   350	    (context.is_scss_exclusive_syntax_allowed() && is_at_any_scss_value(p))
   351	        || is_at_any_css_function(p)
   352	        || is_at_any_non_function_css_value(p)
   353	}
   354	
   355	#[inline]
   356	fn is_at_any_non_function_css_value(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   357	    is_at_identifier(p)
   358	        || p.at(CSS_STRING_LITERAL)
   359	        || is_at_any_dimension(p)
   360	        || p.at(CSS_NUMBER_LITERAL)
   361	        || is_at_dashed_identifier(p)
   362	        || is_at_ratio(p)
   363	        || is_at_color(p)
   364	        || is_at_bracketed_value(p)
   365	        || is_at_metavariable(p)
   366	}
   367	
   368	#[inline]
   369	pub(crate) fn parse_any_value(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   370	    parse_any_value_with_mode(p, ValueParsingMode::ScssAware)
   371	}
   372	
   373	/// Parses any value using CSS-only branches.
   374	///
   375	/// This intentionally skips SCSS-only constructs and is used by
   376	/// `<general-enclosed>` fallback branches.
   377	#[inline]
   378	pub(crate) fn parse_any_css_value(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   379	    parse_any_value_with_mode(p, ValueParsingMode::CssOnly)
   380	}
   381	
   382	#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq)]
   383	pub(crate) enum ValueParsingMode {
   384	    /// Enables SCSS-specific branches where available.
   385	    ScssAware,
   386	    /// Restricts parsing to CSS syntax branches only.
   387	    ///
   388	    /// Used by `<general-enclosed>` fallbacks to avoid SCSS-only diagnostics
   389	    /// for unknown-but-valid CSS constructs.
   390	    CssOnly,
   391	}
   392	
   393	/// Describes how much SCSS-specific behavior a shared value parser may use.
   394	///
   395	/// Shared value parsing distinguishes between:
   396	/// - CSS-only parsing with no SCSS branches
   397	/// - SCSS-exclusive routing for diagnostics and recovery, without committing to
   398	///   full SCSS semantics for ambiguous syntax
   399	/// - full SCSS parsing where ambiguous syntax may be interpreted as SCSS
   400	#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq)]
   401	pub(crate) enum ScssCapability {
   402	    /// Shared parsing must stay on CSS-only branches.
   403	    Disabled,
   404	    /// SCSS-exclusive syntax may be recognized for routing, diagnostics, and
   405	    /// recovery, but ambiguous syntax must not commit to full SCSS semantics.
   406	    ExclusiveOnly,
   407	    /// Full SCSS parsing is enabled.
   408	    Full,
   409	}
   410	
   411	#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq)]
   412	pub(crate) struct ValueParsingContext {
   413	    mode: ValueParsingMode,
   414	    scss_feature_supported: bool,
   415	}
   416	
   417	impl ValueParsingContext {
   418	    #[inline]
   419	    pub(crate) fn new(p: &CssParser, mode: ValueParsingMode) -> Self {
   420	        Self {
   421	            mode,
   422	            scss_feature_supported: CssSyntaxFeatures::Scss.is_supported(p),
   423	        }
   424	    }
   425	
   426	    /// Returns the current SCSS capability level for shared value parsing.
   427	    #[inline]
   428	    pub(crate) const fn scss_capability(self) -> ScssCapability {
   429	        match (self.mode, self.scss_feature_supported) {
   430	            (ValueParsingMode::CssOnly, _) => ScssCapability::Disabled,
   431	            (ValueParsingMode::ScssAware, false) => ScssCapability::ExclusiveOnly,
   432	            (ValueParsingMode::ScssAware, true) => ScssCapability::Full,
   433	        }
   434	    }
   435	
   436	    /// Returns whether SCSS-exclusive branches may be used for routing,
   437	    /// diagnostics, and recovery.
   438	    ///
   439	    /// Use this at mixed CSS/SCSS boundaries when the parser needs to:
   440	    /// - recognize SCSS-only syntax so it can route into
   441	    ///   `parse_exclusive_syntax(...)`
   442	    /// - build a more accurate recovery tree for unsupported SCSS syntax in a
   443	    ///   CSS file
   444	    /// - emit an SCSS-only diagnostic instead of falling through to a generic
   445	    ///   CSS error
   446	    ///
   447	    /// This is the right check for exclusive SCSS forms such as qualified
   448	    /// names, interpolation-led values, or SCSS-only URL modifiers. It should
   449	    /// not be used for ambiguous syntax that shares a token shape with CSS and
   450	    /// needs a stronger semantic commitment.
   451	    #[inline]
   452	    pub(crate) const fn is_scss_exclusive_syntax_allowed(self) -> bool {
   453	        !matches!(self.scss_capability(), ScssCapability::Disabled)
   454	    }
   455	
   456	    /// Returns whether ambiguous syntax may commit to full SCSS semantics.
   457	    ///
   458	    /// Use this only when the parser is about to choose between a CSS
   459	    /// interpretation and a full SCSS interpretation for the same token shape.
   460	    /// Typical examples are:
   461	    /// - CSS `if(...)` versus Sass `if(...)` call disambiguation
   462	    /// - shared function-argument parsing that may need to upgrade into the
   463	    ///   SCSS expression parser
   464	    ///
   465	    /// If the syntax is SCSS-exclusive and does not overlap with CSS parsing,
   466	    /// prefer [`Self::is_scss_exclusive_syntax_allowed`] instead. This helper
   467	    /// is intentionally stricter because it means the caller is committing to
   468	    /// SCSS semantics for ambiguous syntax.
   469	    #[inline]
   470	    pub(crate) const fn is_full_scss_parsing_allowed(self) -> bool {
   471	        matches!(self.scss_capability(), ScssCapability::Full)
   472	    }
   473	}
   474	
   475	/// Parses any value while explicitly controlling whether SCSS-only branches are allowed.
   476	#[inline]
   477	pub(crate) fn parse_any_value_with_mode(p: &mut CssParser, mode: ValueParsingMode) -> ParsedSyntax {
   478	    parse_any_value_with_context(p, ValueParsingContext::new(p, mode))
   479	}
   480	
   481	#[inline]
   482	pub(crate) fn parse_any_value_with_context(
   483	    p: &mut CssParser,
   484	    context: ValueParsingContext,
   485	) -> ParsedSyntax {
   486	    if context.is_scss_exclusive_syntax_allowed() && is_at_any_scss_value(p) {
   487	        parse_any_exclusive_scs<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

    21	impl ParseNodeList for DeclarationList {
    22	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
    23	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
    24	    const LIST_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_DECLARATION_LIST;
    25	
    26	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> ParsedSyntax {
    27	        if is_at_scss_nesting_declaration(p) || is_at_scss_interpolated_property(p) {
    28	            parse_scss_interpolated_property_declaration(p)

    39	        &mut self,
    40	        p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>,

    43	        parsed_element.or_recover_with_token_set(

    60	/// Parses an interpolation-bearing SCSS property in declaration-only contexts.
    61	///
    62	/// Nested-property parsing gets the first chance so `name: { ... }` stays intact.
    63	/// If no nested-property form is found, this falls back to a regular declaration.
    64	#[inline]
    65	pub(crate) fn parse_declaration_with_value_end_set(
    66	    p: &mut CssParser,
    67	    value_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    68	    recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    69	) -> ParsedSyntax {
    70	    parse_declaration_with(p, |p| {
    71	        parse_any_property_with_value_end_set(p, value_end_set, recovery_end_set)
    72	    })
    73	}
    74	
    75	#[inline]
    76	fn parse_declaration_with<F>(p: &mut CssParser, parse_property: F) -> ParsedSyntax
    77	where
    78	    F: FnOnce(&mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax,
    79	{
    80	    let m = p.start();
    81	
    82	    if parse_property(p).is_absent() {
    83	        m.abandon(p);
    84	        return Absent;
    85	    }
    86	    parse_declaration_important(p).ok();
    87	
    88	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_DECLARATION))
    89	}

    91	#[inline]
    92	pub(crate) fn is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
    93	    is_at_any_declaration(p) || is_at_empty_declaration(p)
    94	}
    95	
    96	#[inline]
    97	pub(crate) fn parse_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
    98	    if is_at_empty_declaration(p) {
    99	        parse_empty_declaration(p)
   100	    } else if is_at_scss_declaration(p) {
   101	        CssSyntaxFeatures::Scss.parse_exclusive_syntax(p, parse_scss_declaration, |p, marker| {
   102	            scss_only_syntax_error(p, "SCSS variable declarations", marker.range(p))
   103	        })
   104	    } else if is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p) {
   105	        parse_declaration_with_semicolon(p)
   106	    } else {
   107	        Absent
   108	    }
   109	}
   110	
   111	#[inline]
   112	pub(crate) fn is_at_any_declaration(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   113	    is_at_declaration(p) || is_at_scss_declaration(p)
   114	}
   115	
   116	/// Parses a CSS declaration that may optionally end with a semicolon.
   117	///
   118	/// This function attempts to parse a single CSS declaration from the current position
   119	/// of the parser. It handles the optional semicolon (';') at the end of the declaration,
   120	/// adhering to CSS syntax rules where the semicolon is mandatory for all declarations
   121	/// except the last one in a block. In the case of the last declaration before a closing
   122	/// brace ('}'), the semicolon is optional. If the semicolon is omitted for declarations
   123	/// that are not at the end, the parser will raise an error.
   124	#[inline]
   125	pub(crate) fn parse_declaration_with_semicolon(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   126	    parse_declaration(p).map(|declaration| complete_declaration_with_semicolon(p, declaration))
   127	}
   128	
   129	#[inline]
   130	pub(crate) fn complete_declaration_with_semicolon(
   131	    p: &mut CssParser,
   132	    declaration: CompletedMarker,
   133	) -> CompletedMarker {
   134	    let declaration_with_semicolon = declaration.precede(p);
   135	
   136	    parse_optional_declaration_semicolon(p);
   137	
   138	    declaration_with_semicolon.complete(p, CSS_DECLARATION_WITH_SEMICOLON)
   139	}
   140	
   141	#[inline]
   142	pub(crate) fn parse_optional_declaration_semicolon(p: &mut CssParser) {
   143	    // If the next token is a closing brace ('}'), the semicolon is optional.
   144	    // Otherwise, a semicolon is expected and the parser will enforce its presence.
   145	    // div { color: red; }
   146	    // div { color: red }
   147	    if !p.at(T!['}']) {
   148	        if p.nth_at(1, T!['}']) {
   149	            p.eat(T![;]);
   150	        } else {
   151	            p.expect(T![;]);
   152	        }
   153	    }
   154	}
   155	
   156	#[inline]
   157	pub(crate) fn is_at_empty_declaration(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   158	    p.at(T![;])
   159	}
   160	
   161	#[inline]
   162	pub(crate) fn parse_empty_declaration(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   163	    if !is_at_empty_declaration(p) {
   164	        return Absent;
   165	    }
   166	    let m = p.start();
   167	    p.bump(T![;]);
   168	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_EMPTY_DECLARATION))
   169	}
   170	
   171	#[inline]
   172	pub(crate) fn is_at_declaration_important(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   173	    p.at(T![!]) && p.nth_at(1, T![important])
   174	}
   175	
   176	#[inline]
   177	pub(crate) fn parse_declaration_important(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   178	    if !is_at_declaration_important(p) {
   179	        return Absent;
   180	    }
   181	    let m = p.start();
   182	    p.bump(T![!]);
   183	    p.bump(T![important]);
   184	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_DECLARATION_IMPORTANT))
   185	}

    33	    is_at_generic_property(p)
    34	}

    37	pub(crate) fn parse_any_property(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
    38	    parse_any_property_with_value_end_set(
    39	        p,
    40	        END_OF_PROPERTY_VALUE_COMPONENT_LIST_TOKEN_SET,
    41	        END_OF_PROPERTY_VALUE_TOKEN_SET,
    42	    )

    45	#[inline]
    46	pub(crate) fn parse_any_property_with_value_end_set(
    47	    p: &mut CssParser,
    48	    value_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    49	    recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    50	) -> ParsedSyntax {
    51	    if !is_at_any_property(p) {
    52	        return Absent;
    53	    }
    54	
    55	    match p.cur() {
    56	        T![composes] => {
    57	            parse_composes_property_with_value_end_set(p, value_end_set, recovery_end_set)
    58	        }
    59	        _ => parse_generic_property_with_value_end_set(p, value_end_set, recovery_end_set),
    60	    }
    61	}
    62	
    63	/// Checks if the current parser position is at a `composes` property.
    64	///
    65	/// This function determines if the parser is currently positioned at a `composes` property,
    66	/// which is indicated by the presence of the `composes` keyword followed by a colon (`:`).
    67	#[inline]
    68	fn is_at_composes_property(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
    69	    p.at(T![composes]) && p.nth_at(1, T![:])
    70	}
    71	
    72	/// Parses a `composes` property in CSS Modules.
    73	///
    74	/// This function parses a `composes` property, which is used in CSS Modules to compose classes from other modules.
    75	/// If the current parser position is not at a `composes` property, it returns `Absent`. If CSS Modules are disabled,
    76	/// it generates a diagnostic error and falls back to parsing a generic property.
    77	///
    78	/// Basic usage in CSS:
    79	/// ```css
    80	/// .button {
    81	///     composes: baseButton alertButton from 'base.css';
    82	/// }
    83	///
    84	/// .alert {
    85	///     composes: alertText;
    86	/// }
    87	/// ```
    88	fn parse_composes_property_with_value_end_set(
    89	    p: &mut CssParser,
    90	    value_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    91	    recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    92	) -> ParsedSyntax {
    93	    if !is_at_composes_property(p) {
    94	        return Absent;
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    if CssSyntaxFeatures::CssModules.is_unsupported(p) {
    98	        // `composes` is not a standard CSS feature.
    99	        // Provide a hint on how to enable parsing of the `composes` declaration.
   100	        p.error(composes_not_allowed(p, p.cur_range()));
   101	
   102	        // Fallback to a generic property
   103	        return parse_generic_property_with_value_end_set(p, value_end_set, recovery_end_set);
   104	    }
   105	
   106	    let m = p.start();
   107	    // remap the `composes` keyword to a regular identifier
   108	    parse_regular_identifier(p).ok();
   109	    p.bump(T![:]);
   110	
   111	    {
   112	        let m = p.start();
   113	
   114	        let class_list_end_set = value_end_set.union(token_set!(T![from]));
   115	        let classes = ComposesClassList::new(class_list_end_set, recovery_end_set).parse_list(p);
   116	
   117	        // If the list of classes is empty, generate a diagnostic error.
   118	        if classes.range(p).is_empty() {
   119	            p.error(expected_classes_list(p, p.cur_range()));
   120	        }
   121	
   122	        if p.at(T![from]) {
   123	            let m = p.start();
   124	            p.bump(T![from]);
   125	
   126	            if is_at_identifier(p) {
   127	                parse_regular_identifier(p).ok();
   128	            } else if is_at_string(p) {
   129	                parse_string(p).ok();
   130	            } else {
   131	                p.error(expected_composes_import_source(p, p.cur_range()));
   132	            }
   133	
   134	            m.complete(p, CSS_COMPOSES_IMPORT_SPECIFIER);
   135	        }
   136	        m.complete(p, CSS_COMPOSES_PROPERTY_VALUE);
   137	    }
   138	
   139	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_COMPOSES_PROPERTY))
   140	}
   141	
   142	/// A struct representing a list of classes in a `composes` property.
   143	struct ComposesClassList {
   144	    end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   145	    recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   146	}
   147	
   148	impl ComposesClassList {
   149	    fn new(end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>, recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>) -> Self {
   150	        Self {
   151	            end_set,
   152	            recovery_end_set,
   153	        }
   154	    }
   155	}
   156	
   157	impl ParseNodeList for ComposesClassList {
   158	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   159	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   160	    const LIST_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_COMPOSES_CLASS_LIST;
   161	
   162	    /// Parses an individual element in the `composes` class list.
   163	    ///
   164	    /// This function parses an identifier as a custom identifier because it is a selector,
   165	    /// which is case-sensitive. For more information, see:
   166	    /// https://github.com/css-modules/css-modules/blob/master/docs/composition.md
   167	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> ParsedSyntax {
   168	        parse_custom_identifier_with_keywords(p, CssLexContext::Regular, true)
   169	    }
   170	
   171	    fn is_at_list_end(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   172	        p.at_ts(self.end_set)
   173	    }
   174	
   175	    fn recover(
   176	        &mut self,
   177	        p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>,
   178	        parsed_element: ParsedSyntax,
   179	    ) -> RecoveryResult {
   180	        parsed_element.or_recover(
   181	            p,
   182	            &ComposesClassListParseRecovery::new(self.end_set, self.recovery_end_set),
   183	            expected_identifier,
   184	        )
   185	    }
   186	}
   187	
   188	struct ComposesClassListParseRecovery {
   189	    end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   190	    recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   191	}
   192	
   193	impl ComposesClassListParseRecovery {
   194	    fn new(end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>, recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>) -> Self {
   195	        Self {
   196	            end_set,
   197	            recovery_end_set,
   198	        }
   199	    }
   200	}
   201	
   202	impl ParseRecovery for ComposesClassListParseRecovery {
   203	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   204	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   205	    const RECOVERED_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_BOGUS;
   206	
   207	    fn is_at_recovered(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   208	        // If the next token is the end of the list or the next element, we're at a recovery point.
   209	        p.at_ts(self.end_set) || p.at_ts(self.recovery_end_set) || is_at_identifier(p)
   210	    }
   211	}
   212	
   213	/// Detects the start of a generic property name.
   214	///
   215	/// This covers both direct property names and SCSS interpolation-bearing names:
   216	///
   217	/// ```scss
   218	/// color: red;
   219	/// --color-*: initial;
   220	/// #{$name}: 1px;
   221	/// margin-#{$side}: 1px;
   222	/// ```
   223	#[inline]
   224	pub(crate) fn is_at_generic_property(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   225	    is_at_direct_generic_property(p) || is_at_scss_interpolated_property(p)
   226	}
   227	
   228	/// Detects the direct, non-interpolated property-name forms handled by the
   229	/// generic property parser.
   230	///
   231	/// This includes plain identifiers followed by `:` as well as the Tailwind
   232	/// `--*:` and `--name-*:` theme-reference forms.
   233	#[inline]
   234	fn is_at_direct_generic_property(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   235	    is_nth_at_direct_generic_property(p, 0)
   236	}
   237	
   238	#[inline]
   239	fn is_at_tailwind_theme_reference_property(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   240	    is_nth_at_tailwind_theme_reference_property(p, 0)
   241	}
   242	
   243	#[inline]
   244	pub(crate) fn is_nth_at_direct_generic_property(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   245	    is_nth_at_identifier(p, n)
   246	        && (p.nth_at(n + 1, T![:]) || is_nth_at_tailwind_theme_reference_property(p, n))
   247	}
   248	
   249	#[inline]
   250	fn is_nth_at_tailwind_theme_reference_property(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   251	    // handle --*:
   252	    (p.nth_at(n + 1, T![*]) && p.nth_at(n + 2, T![:]))
   253	        // handle --color-*:
   254	        || (p.nth_at(n + 1, T![-]) && p.nth_at(n + 2, T![*]) && p.nth_at(n + 3, T![:]))
   255	}
   256	
   257	#[inline]
   258	pub(crate) fn parse_generic_property_name(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   259	    if is_at_dashed_identifier(p) && is_at_tailwind_theme_reference_property(p) {
   260	        let Present(ident) = parse_dashed_identifier(p) else {
   261	            return Absent;
   262	        };
   263	
   264	        return if p.at_ts(token_set![T![-], T![*]]) {
   265	            CssSyntaxFeatures::Tailwind.parse_exclusive_syntax(
   266	                p,
   267	                |p| {
   268	                    let m = ident.precede(p);
   269	                    if p.at(T![-]) {
   270	                        p.expect(T![-]);
   271	                    }
   272	                    p.expect(T![*]);
   273	                    Present(m.complete(p, TW_VALUE_THEME_REFERENCE))
   274	                },
   275	                |p, m| tailwind_disabled(p, m.range(p)),
   276	            )
   277	        } else {
   278	            Present(ident)
   279	        };
   280	    }
   281	
   282	    if CssSyntaxFeatures::Scss.is_supported(p) {
   283	        return parse_scss_interpolated_property_name(p).or_else(|| parse_plain_property_name(p));
   284	    }
   285	
   286	    parse_plain_property_name(p)
   287	}
   288	
   289	#[inline]
   290	fn parse_plain_property_name(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   291	    if is_at_dashed_identifier(p) {
   292	        parse_dashed_identifier(p)
   293	    } else {
   294	        parse_regular_identifier(p)
   295	    }
   296	}
   297	
   298	/// Parses a generic property/value pair, using SCSS expression parsing when enabled
   299	/// so lists, maps, and `!important` terminate correctly.
   300	///
   301	/// Example:
   302	/// ```scss
   303	/// margin: 1px 2px, 3px 4px !important;
   304	/// ```
   305	///
   306	/// Docs: https://sass-lang.com/documentation/style-rules/declarations
   307	#[inline]
   308	fn parse_generic_property_with_value_end_set(
   309	    p: &mut CssParser,
   310	    value_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   311	    recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   312	) -> ParsedSyntax {
   313	    let m = p.start();
   314	    if parse_generic_property_name(p).is_absent() {
   315	        m.abandon(p);
   316	        return Absent;
   317	    }
   318	
   319	    p.expect(T![:]);
   320	    parse_property_value_with_end_set(p, value_end_set, recovery_end_set);
   321	
   322	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_GENERIC_PROPERTY))
   323	}
   324	
   325	#[inline]
   326	pub(crate) fn parse_property_value_with_end_set(
   327	    p: &mut CssParser,
   328	    value_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   329	    recovery_end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   330	) {
   331	    if CssSyntaxFeatures::Scss.is_supported(p) {
   332	        parse_required_scss_value_until(p, value_end_set);
   333	    } else {
   334	        GenericComponentValueList::new(value_end_set, recovery_end_set).parse_list(p);
   335	    }
   336	}
   337	
   338	pub(crate) const END_OF_PROPERTY_VALUE_TOKEN_SET: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind> =
   339	    token_set!(T!['}'], T![;]);
   340	// Include `)` to recover malformed values without turning the whole declaration
   341	// into a bogus property, and `!` so `!important` isn't parsed as a value token.
   342	pub(crate) const END_OF_PROPERTY_VALUE_COMPONENT_LIST_TOKEN_SET: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind> =
   343	    END_OF_PROPERTY_VALUE_TOKEN_SET.union(token_set!(T![')'], T![!]));
   344	
   345	pub(crate) struct GenericComponentValueList {
   346	    end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   347	    recovery_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   348	    boundary: Option<fn(&mut CssParser) -> bool>,
   349	}
   350	
   351	impl GenericComponentValueList {
   352	    pub(crate) fn new(
   353	        end_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   354	        recovery_set: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   355	    ) -> Self {
   356	        Self {
   357	            end_set,
   358	            recovery_set,
   359	            boundary: None,
   360	        }
   361	    }
   362	
   363	    pub(crate) fn with_boundary(mut self, boundary: fn(&mut CssParser) -> bool) -> Self {
   364	        self.boundary = Some(boundary);
   365	        self
   366	    }
   367	
   368	    #[inline]
   369	    fn at_boundary(&self, p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   370	        self.boundary.is_some_and(|boundary| boundary(p))
   371	    }
   372	}
   373	
   374	impl Default for GenericComponentValueList {
   375	    fn default() -> Self {
   376	        Self::new(
   377	            END_OF_PROPERTY_VALUE_COMPONENT_LIST_TOKEN_SET,
   378	            END_OF_PROPERTY_VALUE_TOKEN_SET,
   379	        )
   380	    }
   381	}
   382	
   383	impl ParseNodeList for GenericComponentValueList {
   384	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   385	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   386	    const LIST_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_GENERIC_COMPONENT_VALUE_LIST;
   387	
   388	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> ParsedSyntax {
   389	        parse_generic_component_value(p)
   390	    }
   391	
   392	    fn is_at_list_end(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   393	        p.at_ts(self.end_set) || self.at_boundary(p)
   394	    }
   395	
   396	    fn recover(
   397	        &mut self,
   398	        p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>,
   399	        parsed_element: ParsedSyntax,
   400	    ) -> RecoveryResult {
   401	        if self.at_boundary(p) {
   402	            return Err(RecoveryError::AlreadyRecovered);
   403	        }
   404	
   405	        parsed_element.or_recover_with_token_set(
   406	            p,
   407	            &ParseRecoveryTokenSet::new(CSS_BOGUS_PROPERTY_VALUE, self.recovery_set)
   408	                .enable_recovery_on_line_break(),
   409	            expected_component_value,
   410	        )
   411	    }
   412	}
   413	
   414	#[inline]
   415	pub(crate) fn is_at_generic_component_value(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   416	    is_at_any_value(p) || is_at_generic_delimiter(p)
   417	}
   418	
   419	#[inline]
   420	pub(crate) fn parse_generic_component_value(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   421	    if !is_at_generic_component_value(p) {
   422	        return Absent;
   423	    }
   424	
   425	    if is_at_generic_delimiter(p) {
   426	        parse_generic_delimiter(p)
   427	    } else {
   428	        parse_any_value(p)
   429	    }
   430	}
   431	
   432	const GENERIC_DELIMITER_SET: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind> = token_set![T![,], T![/]];
   433	#[inline]
   434	pub(crate) fn is_at_generic_delimiter(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   435	    p.at_ts(GENERIC_DELIMITER_SET)
   436	}
   437	
   438	#[inline]
   439	fn parse_generic_delimiter(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   440	    if !is_at_generic_delimiter(p) {
   441	        return Absent;
   442	    }
   443	
   444	    let m = p.start();
   445	    p.bump_ts(GENERIC_DELIMITER_SET);
   446	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_GENERIC_DELIMITER))
   447	}
   448

2026-06-01 05:29:48,668 - INFO - swea-agent-biomejs__biome-9995 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I understand the issue better. Let me trace through the logic:

In `declaration_or_rule_list_block.rs`, the function `parse_element` handles parsing elements in a `DeclarationOrRuleList`. When we encounter something like `div:first-of-type { ... }`:

1. First, `is_at_declaration_or_rule_item` is checked:
   - `is_at_at_rule(p)` - no
   - `is_at_nested_qualified_rule(p)` - this checks `is_at_relative_selector(p)`. Let me check what that is.
   - `is_at_scss_nesting_declaration(p)` - no (not SCSS)
   - `is_at_scss_declaration(p)` - no
   - `is_at_scss_interpolated_property(p)` - no
   - `is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon(p)` - this checks `is_at_any_declaration(p)` which checks `is_at_declaration(p)` which checks `is_at_any_property(p)` which checks `is_at_generic_property(p)` which checks `is_at_direct_generic_property(p)` which checks `is_nth_at_identifier(p, 0) && p.nth_at(n + 1, T![:])`.

So for `div:first-of-type {`, `div` is an identifier and `:` follows it (as part of `:first-of-type`). So `is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon` returns **true** for `div:first-of-type {` because it sees `div` as identifier and `:` as the colon separating property from value!

That's the root cause! The parser sees `div:first-of-type {` and thinks it's a CSS declaration like `div: first-of-type {` (where `div` is a property and `first-of-type {` is the value).

Now in the `parse_element` function, when `is_at_any_declaration_with_semicolon` is true:
1. It first tries to parse as a declaration
2. The declaration parser expects a property name (`div`) and then `:` and then a value. 
3. The value parsing would try to parse `first-of-type { color: black; }` as a value
4. The value would include the `{` which is not a valid value delimiter
5. After the declaration parse, it checks if `p.last()` is `;` or `}` - it probably isn't
6. So the speculative declaration parse fails
7. Then it tries to parse as a nested qualified rule
8. But wait - the nested qualified rule check uses `is_at_nested_qualified_rule` which checks `is_at_relative_selector`. Let me check that.

Let me look at relative_

    12	/// Represents a list of relative CSS selectors with support for error recovery.
    13	///
    14	/// This structure is used to parse and manage a list of relative CSS selectors
    15	/// within the context of a larger CSS parsing operation. It includes mechanisms
    16	/// for disabling error recovery if needed. For more details on CSS selectors,
    17	/// see the [CSS Selectors Level 4 Specification](https://www.w3.org/TR/selectors-4/#relative).
    18	///
    19	/// ## Example
    20	///
    21	/// A relative selector might look like `"E > F"` where `E` is an element that is
    22	/// a parent of an element `F`. In the context of this structure, a list might be
    23	/// parsed from a string like `"div > p, ul > li:first-child"`.
    24	pub(crate) struct RelativeSelectorList {
    25	    /// The type of CSS syntax that marks the end of this selector list.
    26	    end_kind: CssSyntaxKind,
    27	    /// Flag indicating whether error recovery is disabled.
    28	    is_recovery_disabled: bool,
    29	}
    30	
    31	impl RelativeSelectorList {
    32	    /// Constructs a new `RelativeSelectorList`.
    33	    ///
    34	    /// Initializes the list with a specified end kind, with error recovery enabled by default.
    35	    ///
    36	    /// # Arguments
    37	    ///
    38	    /// * `end_kind` - A `CssSyntaxKind` that indicates the end of the selector list.
    39	    pub(crate) fn new(end_kind: CssSyntaxKind) -> Self {
    40	        Self {
    41	            end_kind,
    42	            is_recovery_disabled: false,
    43	        }
    44	    }
    45	
    46	    /// Disables error recovery for this selector list.
    47	    ///
    48	    /// This method modifies the instance to disable the error recovery mechanism.
    49	    /// It is useful when strict parsing is required without fallbacks.
    50	    pub(crate) fn disable_recovery(mut self) -> Self {
    51	        self.is_recovery_disabled = true;
    52	        self
    53	    }
    54	}
    55	
    56	/// Defines error recovery behavior for parsing `RelativeSelectorList`.
    57	///
    58	/// This structure is utilized when the parser encounters errors while parsing
    59	/// a list of relative CSS selectors, allowing it to recover and continue.
    60	pub(crate) struct RelativeSelectorListParseRecovery {
    61	    /// The type of CSS syntax that indicates a potential recovery point.
    62	    end_kind: CssSyntaxKind,
    63	}
    64	
    65	impl RelativeSelectorListParseRecovery {
    66	    /// Constructs a new `RelativeSelectorListParseRecovery`.
    67	    ///
    68	    /// Initializes the recovery mechanism with a specified end kind.
    69	    ///
    70	    /// # Arguments
    71	    ///
    72	    /// * `end_kind` - A `CssSyntaxKind` used to determine the recovery point.
    73	    pub(crate) fn new(end_kind: CssSyntaxKind) -> Self {
    74	        Self { end_kind }
    75	    }
    76	}
    77	
    78	impl ParseRecovery for RelativeSelectorListParseRecovery {
    79	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
    80	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
    81	    const RECOVERED_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_BOGUS_SELECTOR;
    82	
    83	    /// Determines if the current parser position is a point of recovery.
    84	    ///
    85	    /// Recovery is attempted if the current position matches the `end_kind`,
    86	    /// a comma, or is identified as a relative selector by additional checks.
    87	    /// This ensures parsing can resume smoothly after an error, by identifying
    88	    /// specific tokens or conditions that indicate a safe point for continuation.
    89	    ///
    90	    /// # CSS Examples
    91	    ///
    92	    /// - Recovery at `end_kind` (assuming `end_kind` is `{` for demonstration):
    93	    ///   ```css
    94	    ///   .class1 , 12312312                    {
    95	    ///   /*        ^^^^^^^^ invalid selector   ^ recovery point at end_kind */
    96	    ///   ```
    97	    ///
    98	    /// - Recovery at Comma (`,`) between selectors:
    99	    ///   ```css
   100	    ///   .class1 , 12312312                    , .class3 {
   101	    ///   /*        ^^^^^^^^ invalid selector   ^ recovery point at comma */
   102	    ///   ```
   103	    ///
   104	    /// - Recovery at Relative Selector:
   105	    ///   - When the parser identifies a combinator indicating the start of a new relative selector:
   106	    ///     ```css
   107	    ///     .class1 , 12312312                      > .class2 {
   108	    ///     /*        ^^^^^^^^ invalid selector     ^ recovery point at new relative selector */
   109	    ///     ```
   110	    fn is_at_recovered(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   111	        p.at(self.end_kind) || p.at(T![,]) || is_at_relative_selector(p)
   112	    }
   113	}
   114	
   115	impl ParseSeparatedList for RelativeSelectorList {
   116	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   117	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   118	
   119	    const LIST_KIND: CssSyntaxKind = CSS_RELATIVE_SELECTOR_LIST;
   120	
   121	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   122	        parse_relative_selector(p)
   123	    }
   124	
   125	    fn is_at_list_end(&self, p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   126	        p.at(self.end_kind)
   127	    }
   128	
   129	    fn recover(&mut self, p: &mut CssParser, parsed_element: ParsedSyntax) -> RecoveryResult {
   130	        if parsed_element.is_absent() && self.is_recovery_disabled {
   131	            Err(RecoveryError::RecoveryDisabled)
   132	        } else {
   133	            parsed_element.or_recover(
   134	                p,
   135	                &RelativeSelectorListParseRecovery::new(self.end_kind),
   136	                expected_relative_selector,
   137	            )
   138	        }
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    fn separating_element_kind(&mut self) -> CssSyntaxKind {
   142	        T![,]
   143	    }
   144	}
   145	
   146	/// A set of CSS syntax kinds representing combinator types for relative selectors.
   147	///
   148	/// This constant defines a set of combinators used in CSS to establish relationships
   149	/// between selectors. It includes combinators like `>`, `+`, `~`, and `||`, which
   150	/// are used to specify parent-child, adjacent siblings, general siblings, and column
   151	/// combinator selectors, respectively.
   152	const RELATIVE_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind> =
   153	    token_set![T![>], T![+], T![~], T![||]];
   154	
   155	/// Checks if the current position in the parser is at a relative selector combinator.
   156	///
   157	/// This function uses `RELATIVE_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET` to determine if the current
   158	/// position in the CSS parser is at a combinator that can be used in a relative selector.
   159	#[inline]
   160	fn is_at_relative_selector_combinator(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   161	    p.at_ts(RELATIVE_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET)
   162	}
   163	
   164	/// Determines if the parser is positioned at a relative selector.
   165	///
   166	/// This function checks if the current position in the CSS parser is at a relative selector
   167	/// by checking for the presence of a combinator or if it's at the start of a compound selector.
   168	#[inline]
   169	pub(crate) fn is_at_relative_selector(p: &mut CssParser) -> bool {
   170	    is_at_relative_selector_combinator(p) || is_nth_at_compound_selector(p, 0)
   171	}
   172	
   173	/// Parses a relative selector from the current position in the CSS parser.
   174	///
   175	/// This function attempts to parse a relative selector starting from the current position. If the position
   176	/// does not indicate a relative selector, it returns `Absent`. Otherwise, it parses the selector, adding
   177	/// diagnostics if necessary, and returns `Present` with the parsed syntax.
   178	#[inline]
   179	fn parse_relative_selector(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   180	    if !is_at_relative_selector(p) {
   181	        return Absent;
   182	    }
   183	
   184	    let m = p.start();
   185	
   186	    // Consume the combinator from the set of relative selector combinators.
   187	    p.eat_ts(RELATIVE_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET);
   188	    // Attempt to parse the selector, adding a diagnostic if it fails.
   189	    parse_selector(p).or_add_diagnostic(p, expected_relative_selector);
   190	
   191	    // Mark the end of the parsing operation and return the result.
   192	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_RELATIVE_SELECTOR))
   193	}
   194

    25	    parse_custom_identifier_with_keywords, parse_identifier, parse_regular_identifier,
    26	};
    27	use biome_css_syntax::CssSyntaxKind::*;
    28	use biome_css_syntax::{CssSyntaxKind, T, TextRange};
    29	use biome_parser::diagnostic::ToDiagnostic;
    30	use biome_parser::parse_lists::{ParseNodeList, ParseSeparatedList};
    31	use biome_parser::parse_recovery::{
    32	    ParseRecovery, ParseRecoveryTokenSet, RecoveryError, RecoveryResult,
    33	};
    34	use biome_parser::prelude::ParsedSyntax;
    35	use biome_parser::prelude::ParsedSyntax::{Absent, Present};
    36	use biome_parser::{CompletedMarker, Parser, ParserProgress, SyntaxFeature, TokenSet, token_set};
    37	
    38	/// Determines the lexical context for parsing CSS selectors.
    39	///
    40	/// This function is applied when lexing CSS selectors. It decides whether the
    41	/// current context should be treated as a regular context or a selector-specific
    42	/// context. The distinction is important for handling whitespaces, especially
    43	/// around combinators in CSS selectors.
    44	const SELECTOR_LEX_SET: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind> =
    45	    COMPLEX_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET.union(token_set![T!['{'], T![,], T![')'], T![!], T![;], EOF]);
    46	#[inline]
    47	pub(crate) fn selector_lex_context(p: &mut CssParser) -> CssLexContext {
    48	    // It's an inverted logic for `is_nth_at_selector(p, 1)`.
    49	    if p.nth_at_ts(1, SELECTOR_LEX_SET) {
    50	        CssLexContext::Regular
    51	    } else {
    52	        CssLexContext::Selector
    53	    }
    54	}
    55	
    56	pub(crate) struct SelectorList {
    57	    end_kind_ts: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    58	    recovery_ts: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
    59	    is_recovery_disabled: bool,
    60	}
    61	
    62	impl Default for SelectorList {
    63	    fn default() -> Self {
    64	        Self {
    65	            end_kind_ts: token_set!(T!['{']),
    66	            recovery_ts: token_set![T!['{']],
    67	            is_recovery_disabled: false,
    68	        }
    69	    }
    70	}
    71	
    72	impl SelectorList {
    73	    /// Configures the `SelectorList` with a specified token set to indicate the end of the selector list.
    74	    ///
    75	    /// This method allows setting a custom `TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>` that determines the tokens
    76	    /// which mark the end of a selector list. It can be used to extend or modify the default
    77	    /// behavior of the selector list parsing.
    78	    pub(crate) fn with_end_kind_ts(mut self, end_kind_ts: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>) -> Self {
    79	        self.end_kind_ts = end_kind_ts;
    80	        self
    81	    }
    82	
    83	    /// Configures the `SelectorList` with a specified set of tokens for error recovery.
    84	    ///
    85	    /// This method allows setting a custom `TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>` which is used for
    86	    /// identifying points at which the parser can attempt to recover from errors during
    87	    /// the parsing process. The specified token set represents tokens that the parser will
    88	    /// recognize as potential recovery points.
    89	    pub(crate) fn with_recovery_ts(mut self, recovery_ts: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>) -> Self {
    90	        self.recovery_ts = recovery_ts;
    91	        self
    92	    }
    93	
    94	    /// Disables error recovery for the selector list parsing.
    95	    ///
    96	    /// By default, the parser might attempt to recover from errors encountered while parsing
    97	    /// the selector list. This method disables such recovery, which can be useful in scenarios
    98	    /// where we want to implement a custom recovery.
    99	    pub(crate) fn disable_recovery(mut self) -> Self {
   100	        self.is_recovery_disabled = true;
   101	        self
   102	    }
   103	}
   104	
   105	impl ParseSeparatedList for SelectorList {
   106	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   107	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   108	    const LIST_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_SELECTOR_LIST;
   109	
   110	    fn parse_element(&mut self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> ParsedSyntax {
   111	        parse_selector(p)
   112	    }
   113	
   114	    fn is_at_list_end(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   115	        p.at_ts(self.end_kind_ts) || p.at(CSS_SPACE_LITERAL) && p.nth_at_ts(1, self.end_kind_ts)
   116	    }
   117	
   118	    fn recover(
   119	        &mut self,
   120	        p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>,
   121	        parsed_element: ParsedSyntax,
   122	    ) -> RecoveryResult {
   123	        if parsed_element.is_absent() && self.is_recovery_disabled {
   124	            p.error(expected_selector(p, p.cur_range()));
   125	            Err(RecoveryError::RecoveryDisabled)
   126	        } else {
   127	            parsed_element.or_recover(
   128	                p,
   129	                &SelectorListParseRecovery::new(self.recovery_ts),
   130	                expected_selector,
   131	            )
   132	        }
   133	    }
   134	
   135	    fn separating_element_kind(&mut self) -> Self::Kind {
   136	        T![,]
   137	    }
   138	}
   139	
   140	struct SelectorListParseRecovery {
   141	    recovery_ts: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>,
   142	}
   143	
   144	impl SelectorListParseRecovery {
   145	    fn new(recovery_ts: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind>) -> Self {
   146	        Self { recovery_ts }
   147	    }
   148	}
   149	
   150	impl ParseRecovery for SelectorListParseRecovery {
   151	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   152	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   153	    const RECOVERED_KIND: Self::Kind = CSS_BOGUS_SELECTOR;
   154	
   155	    /// Determines if the parser is at a point where it can recover from an error
   156	    /// while parsing a selector list.
   157	    ///
   158	    /// This method checks if the parser is currently positioned at a token that
   159	    /// indicates a safe point to resume parsing after encountering an error in a
   160	    /// selector list. The recovery points are identified by `recovery_ts`, a comma,
   161	    /// the start of a new selector, or a preceding line break.
   162	    /// # CSS Examples
   163	    ///
   164	    /// - Recovery at `recovery_ts` (e.g., `{`):
   165	    ///   ```css
   166	    ///   .class1, { }
   167	    ///   /*       ^                   */
   168	    ///   /*       is a recovery point */
   169	    ///   ```
   170	    ///
   171	    /// - Recovery at Comma (`,`):
   172	    ///   ```css
   173	    ///   .class1, "string", div {}
   174	    ///   /*               ^                   */
   175	    ///   /*               is a recovery point */
   176	    ///   ```
   177	    ///
   178	    /// - Recovery at New Selector (`is_nth_at_selector`):
   179	    ///   ```css
   180	    ///   .class1, "string" div {}
   181	    ///   /*                ^                   */
   182	    ///   /*                is a recovery point */
   183	    ///   ```
   184	    ///
   185	    /// - Recovery at Line Break (`has_nth_preceding_line_break`):
   186	    ///   ```css
   187	    ///   .class1 123123
   188	    ///    /*new line is a recovery point*/  color: red;
   189	    ///   }
   190	    ///   ```
   191	    ///
   192	    fn is_at_recovered(&self, p: &mut Self::Parser<'_>) -> bool {
   193	        p.at_ts(self.recovery_ts)
   194	            || p.at(T![,])
   195	            || is_nth_at_selector(p, 0)
   196	            || p.has_nth_preceding_line_break(1)
   197	    }
   198	}
   199	
   200	/// Determines if the current or nth token in the parser is at the start of a selector.
   201	///
   202	/// This function checks whether the specified position in the CSS parser aligns with
   203	/// the beginning of a compound selector. In CSS, selectors are patterns used to select
   204	/// the elements to which a set of CSS rules apply.
   205	#[inline]
   206	pub(crate) fn is_nth_at_selector(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   207	    is_nth_at_compound_selector(p, n) || is_nth_at_metavariable(p, n)
   208	}
   209	
   210	/// Parses a CSS selector.
   211	///
   212	/// This function attempts to parse a CSS selector, which may be either a compound selector
   213	/// or a complex selector. Compound selectors are simple, unseparated chains of simple selectors,
   214	/// whereas complex selectors are compound selectors separated by combinators.
   215	///
   216	/// Initially, the function tries to parse a compound selector. If successful, it then checks
   217	/// if this compound selector forms part of a complex selector and continues parsing accordingly.
   218	#[inline]
   219	pub(crate) fn parse_selector(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   220	    if !is_nth_at_selector(p, 0) {
   221	        return Absent;
   222	    }
   223	    if is_nth_at_metavariable(p, 0) {
   224	        parse_metavariable(p)
   225	    } else {
   226	        // In CSS, we have compound selectors and complex selectors.
   227	        // Compound selectors are simple, unseparated chains of selectors,
   228	        // while complex selectors are compound selectors separated by combinators.
   229	        // After parsing the compound selector, it then checks if this compound selector is a part of a complex selector.
   230	        parse_compound_selector(p).and_then(|selector| parse_complex_selector(p, selector))
   231	    }
   232	}
   233	
   234	const COMPLEX_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind> =
   235	    token_set![T![>], T![+], T![~], T![||], CSS_SPACE_LITERAL];
   236	
   237	/// Checks if the current or nth token in the parser is a complex selector combinator.
   238	///
   239	/// This function determines whether the specified position in the CSS parser is at a
   240	/// complex selector combinator. Complex selector combinators include characters like
   241	/// '>', '+', ' ', and '~', used to define relationships between different elements in CSS.
   242	#[inline]
   243	fn is_nth_at_complex_selector_combinator(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   244	    p.nth_at_ts(n, COMPLEX_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET)
   245	}
   246	
   247	/// Parses a complex selector in CSS.
   248	///
   249	/// This function attempts to parse a complex selector, which combines compound selectors
   250	/// through combinators like '>', '+', ' ', and '~'. These combinators express relationships
   251	/// between elements in the document tree.
   252	///
   253	/// The function iterates over the tokens, parsing each complex selector combinator and
   254	/// its associated compound selector. Parsing continues until no more complex combinators
   255	/// are found, at which point it returns the completed complex selector.
   256	#[inline]
   257	fn parse_complex_selector(p: &mut CssParser, mut left: CompletedMarker) -> ParsedSyntax {
   258	    let mut progress = ParserProgress::default();
   259	
   260	    loop {
   261	        progress.assert_progressing(p);
   262	
   263	        if is_nth_at_complex_selector_combinator(p, 0) {
   264	            let complex_selector = left.precede(p);
   265	
   266	            p.bump_ts(COMPLEX_SELECTOR_COMBINATOR_SET);
   267	            parse_compound_selector(p).or_add_diagnostic(p, expected_compound_selector);
   268	            left = complex_selector.complete(p, CSS_COMPLEX_SELECTOR)
   269	        } else {
   270	            return Present(left);
   271	        }
   272	    }
   273	}
   274	
   275	/// Determines if the current or nth token in the parser is at the start of a compound selector.
   276	///
   277	/// This function checks if the specified position in the CSS parser corresponds to the start
   278	/// of a compound selector. A compound selector combines multiple simple selectors, optionally
   279	/// including a reference combinator ('&').
   280	#[inline]
   281	fn is_nth_at_compound_selector(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   282	    p.nth_at(n, T![&])
   283	        || is_nth_at_simple_selector(p, n)
   284	        || p.nth_at_ts(n, SubSelectorList::START_SET)
   285	}
   286	
   287	/// Parses a compound selector in CSS.
   288	///
   289	/// This function attempts to parse a compound selector from the current position in the
   290	/// CSS parser. Compound selectors are combinations of simple selectors and optionally
   291	/// a reference combinator ('&'). They match elements based on multiple conditions.
   292	#[inline]
   293	fn parse_compound_selector(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   294	    if !is_nth_at_compound_selector(p, 0) {
   295	        return Absent;
   296	    }
   297	
   298	    let m = p.start();
   299	
   300	    NestedSelectorList.parse_list(p);
   301	    parse_simple_selector(p).ok(); // We don't need to handle error here because a simple selector is optional
   302	    SubSelectorList.parse_list(p);
   303	
   304	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_COMPOUND_SELECTOR))
   305	}
   306	
   307	/// Checks if the current or nth token in the parser is a simple selector.
   308	///
   309	/// This function determines if the specified token position in the CSS parser is at the start
   310	/// of a simple selector. Simple selectors are the basic building blocks of CSS selectors,
   311	/// including type selectors, universal selectors, and attribute selectors.
   312	#[inline]
   313	fn is_nth_at_simple_selector(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   314	    is_nth_at_namespace(p, n)
   315	        || p.nth_at(n, T![*])
   316	        || is_nth_at_identifier(p, n)
   317	        || is_nth_at_scss_interpolated_identifier(p, n)
   318	        || is_nth_at_scss_placeholder_selector(p, n)
   319	}
   320	
   321	/// Parses a simple selector in CSS.
   322	///
   323	/// This function attempts to parse a simple selector from the current position in the CSS parser.
   324	/// Simple selectors are the most basic component of CSS selectors, including type selectors,
   325	/// universal selectors, and attribute selectors.
   326	#[inline]
   327	fn parse_simple_selector(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   328	    if !is_nth_at_simple_selector(p, 0) {
   329	        return Absent;
   330	    }
   331	
   332	    if is_nth_at_scss_placeholder_selector(p, 0) {
   333	        return parse_scss_placeholder_selector(p);
   334	    }
   335	
   336	    let namespace = parse_namespace(p);
   337	
   338	    if p.at(T![*]) {
   339	        parse_universal_selector(p, namespace)
   340	    } else {
   341	        parse_type_selector(p, namespace)
   342	    }
   343	}
   344	
   345	/// Determines if the current or nth token in the parser is at a namespace or namespace prefix.
   346	///
   347	/// This function checks if the specified token position in the CSS parser is at a namespace
   348	/// delimiter ('|') or at a namespace prefix. A namespace in CSS is used to differentiate
   349	/// between different types of elements and attributes.
   350	#[inline]
   351	fn is_nth_at_namespace(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   352	    p.nth_at(n, T![|]) || is_nth_at_namespace_prefix(p, n) && p.nth_at(n + 1, T![|])
   353	}
   354	
   355	/// Parses a namespace declaration in CSS.
   356	///
   357	/// This function attempts to parse a namespace declaration from the current position
   358	/// in the CSS parser. Namespaces in CSS are used for qualifying element and attribute
   359	/// names by associating them with a namespace, defined by a URI.
   360	#[inline]
   361	fn parse_namespace(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   362	    if !is_nth_at_namespace(p, 0) {
   363	        return Absent;
   364	    }
   365	
   366	    let m = p.start();
   367	
   368	    // we don't need diagnostic here, because prefix is optional
   369	    parse_namespace_prefix(p).ok();
   370	    p.bump(T![|]);
   371	
   372	    Present(m.complete(p, CSS_NAMESPACE))
   373	}
   374	
   375	/// Checks if the current or nth token in the parser is a namespace prefix.
   376	///
   377	/// This function determines whether the parser is currently positioned at a namespace
   378	/// prefix, either a universal namespace prefix ('*') or a specific identifier.
   379	/// Namespace prefixes in CSS are used in conjunction with namespace URIs to apply
   380	/// styles to elements in that namespace.
   381	#[inline]
   382	fn is_nth_at_namespace_prefix(p: &mut CssParser, n: usize) -> bool {
   383	    p.nth_at(n, T![*]) || is_nth_at_identifier(p, n)
   384	}
   385	
   386	/// Parses a namespace prefix in CSS.
   387	///
   388	/// This function attempts to parse a namespace prefix, which can be a universal
   389	/// namespace prefix ('*') or a named namespace prefix (an identifier). The namespace
   390	/// prefix is used in CSS selectors to apply styles to elements within a specific namespace.
   391	#[inline]
   392	fn parse_namespace_prefix(p: &mut CssParser) -> ParsedSyntax {
   393	    if !is_nth_at_namespace_prefix(p, 0) {
   394	        return Absent;
   395	    }
   396	
   397	    let m = p.start();
   398	
   399	    let kind = if p.eat(T![*]) {
   400	        CSS_UNIVERSAL_NAMESPACE_PREFIX
   401	    } else {
   402	        // we don't need to check if the identifier is valid, because we already did that
   403	        parse_regular_identifier(p).ok();
   404	        CSS_NAMED_NAMESPACE_PREFIX
   405	    };
   406	
   407	    Present(m.complete(p, kind))
   408	}
   409	
   410	pub(crate) struct SubSelectorList;
   411	impl SubSelectorList {
   412	    pub(crate) const START_SET: TokenSet<CssSyntaxKind> =
   413	        token_set![T![#], T![.], T![:], T![::], T!['[']];
   414	}
   415	impl ParseNodeList for SubSelectorList {
   416	    type Kind = CssSyntaxKind;
   417	    type Parser<'source> = CssParser<'source>;
   418	
   419	    const LIST_KIND: CssSyntaxKind = CSS_SUB_SELECTOR_LIS<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. This is intentional for cases like `label:hover {` where it looks like a declaration but is actually a nested rule.\n\nThe issue is in the speculative parsing logic.
